target_role.title: Medior ML Engineer
gaps: Separation of domain logic from presentation concerns

strengths.evidence: Resume lists Python as 'very strong'; all PRs and projects demonstrate fluent, idiomatic Python usage.
Resume highlights RAG, transformers, embeddings, Agentic AI, and OpenAI SDK; Atlas project involved recommendation ranking with ML.
Experience with Docker, CI/CD, Terraform, Kubernetes, MLflow; contributed to Helios deployment patterns.
Certified Azure AI Engineer Associate and GCP Professional Data Engineer; hands-on experience across GCP, Azure, and AWS.
Strong FastAPI / Pydantic / OpenAPI skills from resume; PR #142 showed good instincts for explicit API contracts.
Coaching notes praise readable implementation style; PR #88 reviewer appreciated local runbook documentation.

growth_areas.evidence: Coaching note: 'tends to jump into solution mode before fully framing boundaries and tradeoffs.' Reviewer feedback on PR #142: should separate domain logic from response formatting.
PR #88 review: exception handling silently advances checkpoint, no metrics on processed records. Coaching note: 'underestimates production failure modes, especially in batch jobs.'
PR #142: ranker should not format user-facing text. PR #57: OnboardingPage component doing too much (file parsing, form state, UI transitions).
Coaching note: 'strengthen communication around technical rationale and delivery decisions.' Advised to present risks and deliberate compromises.
PR #57 review: 'should continue developing stronger componentization habits.' Hardcoded labels flagged as a maintenance risk.

onboarding_quiz_answers.biggest_challenge: translating local work into production-ready systems
onboarding_quiz_answers.motivation: grow into a mid-level engineer with broader ownership

internal.name: Confluence
internal.description: Project documentation, architecture reviews, and design decision records from Atlas, Helios, and Orion.
internal.impact: Provides context on real project delivery decisions and best practices Kjell can learn from.

internal.name: GitHub PRs
internal.description: Pull request reviews with line-level feedback from tech leads and senior engineers.
internal.impact: Direct evidence of coding strengths and improvement areas from peer review.

internal.name: Coaching Notes
internal.description: Weekly mentorship and technical coaching session summaries from Sanne Verbruggen and Eline Martens.
internal.impact: Personalized growth observations, actionable advice, and next steps from mentors.

internal.name: Google Drive
internal.description: Company-wide playbooks and guidelines on service design, MLOps observability, frontend AI patterns, and code review.
internal.impact: Curated learning resources aligned with identified growth areas.

internal.name: Resume / CV
internal.description: Uploaded CV with work experience, certifications, education, and self-reported skill levels.
internal.impact: Baseline skill detection and career context for growth path generation.

growth_path.title: Junior to Mid-level ML Engineer Growth Path

areas.name: Architectural Thinking
areas.description: Develop the habit of framing design boundaries, tradeoffs, and failure cases before jumping into implementation.

tasks.title: Write a design note with 3 bullets: responsibility, boundaries, failure cases
why: Coaching feedback: you tend to jump into solution mode before framing boundaries.
Atlas project showed that documenting design decisions early builds client trust.
what_you_will_achieve: A reusable template for lightweight design documentation.
Practice articulating tradeoffs before committing to an implementation.

tasks.title: Lead a short design walkthrough for the next internal feature
why: Direct mentor recommendation from Sanne: 'Lead a short design walkthrough for the next internal feature.'
Practicing verbal articulation of design rationale builds stakeholder communication skills.
what_you_will_achieve: Confidence presenting technical decisions to a team.
Feedback on how your design reasoning is received.

external.label: How to Run a Design Review

tasks.title: Refactor PR #142: separate ranker logic from response formatting
why: PR #142 review flagged that the ranker should not directly format user-facing explanation text.
Practicing separation of concerns in a real codebase solidifies the concept.
what_you_will_achieve: A cleaner service boundary between ranking logic and API presentation.
A concrete example of applying the separation of concerns principle.

areas.name: Production Observability & Reliability
areas.description: Build the instinct to think about monitoring, rollback, and failure handling as first-class concerns, not afterthoughts.

tasks.title: Add observability criteria to your personal implementation checklist
why: Eline's coaching note: 'Add observability criteria to personal implementation checklist.'
MLOps Playbook highlights that observability should be instrumented before failures happen.
what_you_will_achieve: A personal checklist ensuring you always consider monitoring from the start.
Fewer blind spots in production deployments.

tasks.title: Fix PR #88: make checkpoint fail-safe and add processing metrics
why: PR #88 review: exception handling advances the checkpoint even on failure, silently dropping data.
Reviewer asked for metrics on processed records and failures.
what_you_will_achieve: A fail-safe checkpoint mechanism that does not lose data on errors.
Production-grade metrics for batch job visibility.

internal.label: Helios deployment pattern - model version metadata

tasks.title: Review one production incident postmortem and summarize lessons
why: Eline recommended reviewing a postmortem to internalize real-world failure patterns.
Understanding past incidents builds intuition for defensive engineering.
what_you_will_achieve: A written summary of failure patterns and prevention strategies.
Better instincts for anticipating production issues.

external.label: How to Write a Great Postmortem

areas.name: Delivery & Stakeholder Communication
areas.description: Grow from a strong individual contributor into someone who communicates technical rationale, risks, and tradeoffs effectively to the team and stakeholders.

tasks.title: Present a completed feature with a section on risks and deliberate compromises
why: Sanne's advice: 'When presenting work, include one section on risks and one on deliberate compromises.'
Atlas lessons learned show that clients respond positively to transparent tradeoff documentation.
what_you_will_achieve: Practice structuring a technical presentation around decision rationale.
Build a habit of including risk and compromise sections in feature handoffs.

external.label: Staff Engineer: Leadership beyond the management track (excerpt)

tasks.title: Speak up with an idea in the next planning session, even if incomplete
why: Sanne's advice: 'Start speaking up earlier in planning conversations, even with incomplete ideas.'
Developing this habit is key for transitioning to a mid-level role.
what_you_will_achieve: Confidence contributing early in group design discussions.
Feedback on how your ideas land with the team.

external.label: The Art of Speaking Up in Meetings

tasks.title: Own a small feature end-to-end and present the decision rationale to the team
why: Direct next step from Sanne's career growth check-in.
End-to-end ownership is the clearest signal of mid-level readiness.
what_you_will_achieve: Demonstrated ability to own scope from design to delivery.
A concrete example of mid-level contribution for career progression.

external.label: On Owning Your Work as an Engineer

activity_log.details: Onboarding session started. Resume uploaded and skills detected.
Onboarding completed. Growth path generated based on resume, coaching notes, and PR reviews.
Started working on design note template exercise.
Completed design note with responsibility, boundaries, and failure cases for the mentor-assistant feature.
Started creating personal observability implementation checklist.
Observability checklist added to personal Notion. Includes metrics, alerts, and rollback checks.
Prepared feature presentation with risks and compromises section.
Presented onboarding flow feature to the team. Included a section on risks (hardcoded labels) and compromises (monolithic component for speed).
Began preparing design walkthrough for the next internal feature.
Started fixing fail-safe checkpoint and adding processing metrics to PR #88.
Bonus XP for maintaining a 5-day streak.
